annotations.label: General linear group (degree n)

anchor.snippet: the general linear group of degree

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup n R := (Matrix n n R)ˣ` with notation `GL` directly captures GL of degree n.

annotations.label: General linear group over a field/ring

anchor.snippet: More generally, the general linear group of degree

annotations.note: Defined for any `Semiring R`, so covers GL(n, F) for fields and GL(n, R) for rings.

annotations.label: General linear group of a vector space

anchor.snippet: More generally still, the general linear group of a vector space

annotations.note: `LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup R M := (M →ₗ[R] M)ˣ` defines GL(V) for a module/vector space.

annotations.label: Special linear group

anchor.snippet: The special linear group , written

annotations.note: `Matrix.SpecialLinearGroup n R := { A : Matrix n n R // A.det = 1 }` with notation `SL(n, R)`.

annotations.label: GL(n,F) is non-abelian

anchor.snippet: then the group

annotations.note: No explicit lemma stating non-commutativity of GL(n,F) for n ≥ 2 was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: GL(V) as automorphism group

anchor.section: General linear group of a vector space
anchor.snippet: the group of all automorphisms of

annotations.note: `generalLinearEquiv R M : GeneralLinearGroup R M ≃* M ≃ₗ[R] M` identifies GL(V) with the group of linear automorphisms.

annotations.label: GL(V) ≅ GL(n,F) via basis choice

anchor.section: General linear group of a vector space
anchor.snippet: are isomorphic . The isomorphism is not canonical

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup.toLin' b : GL n R ≃* LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup R V` gives the basis-dependent (non-canonical) isomorphism.

annotations.label: GL of a free module

anchor.section: General linear group of a vector space
anchor.snippet: In a similar way, for a commutative ring

annotations.note: `LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup R M` is defined for any semiring R and module M, generalizing the free-module case.

annotations.label: Invertibility over a field via determinant

anchor.snippet: a matrix is invertible if and only if its determinant is nonzero

annotations.note: `mkOfDetNeZero` builds a GL element from any matrix over a field with nonzero determinant; combined with `det_ne_zero` this gives the iff.

annotations.label: Invertibility over a commutative ring

anchor.snippet: a matrix over

annotations.note: `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det : IsUnit A ↔ IsUnit A.det` over a commutative ring.

annotations.label: GL over non-commutative ring

anchor.snippet: Over a non-commutative ring

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup n R := (Matrix n n R)ˣ` is defined for any `Semiring R`, so it works over non-commutative rings.

annotations.label: GL(n,R) is a real Lie group of dimension n²

anchor.snippet: over the field of real numbers is a real Lie group of dimension

annotations.note: No `LieGroup`/`ChartedSpace`/smooth manifold instance for `GL n ℝ` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lie algebra of GL(n,R)

anchor.snippet: The Lie algebra of

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration identifies `Matrix n n ℝ` (with bracket) as the Lie algebra of `GL n ℝ`.

annotations.label: GL(n,R) has two connected components

anchor.snippet: is not connected but rather has two connected components

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Matrix.GLPos` (positive-determinant subgroup) but has no theorem about the number of connected components of `GL n ℝ`.

annotations.label: Polar decomposition homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: The polar decomposition , which is unique for invertible matrices

annotations.note: No polar-decomposition homeomorphism of GL(n,ℝ) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal compact subgroup of GL(n,R)

anchor.snippet: maximal compact subgroup of

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Matrix.orthogonalGroup` but no statement that O(n) is a maximal compact subgroup of GL(n,ℝ).

annotations.label: Fundamental group of GL(n,R)

anchor.snippet: is not simply connected

annotations.note: No computation of π₁(GL(n,ℝ)) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: GL(n,C) is a complex Lie group of dimension n²

anchor.snippet: is a complex Lie group of complex dimension

annotations.note: No complex Lie group / complex manifold instance on `GL n ℂ` was found.

annotations.label: Complex matrices as real matrices preserving a complex structure

anchor.snippet: can be characterized as real

annotations.note: No statement characterizing GL(n,ℂ) as a subgroup of GL(2n,ℝ) preserving a complex structure was found.

annotations.label: Lie algebra of GL(n,C)

anchor.snippet: The Lie algebra corresponding to

annotations.note: No declaration identifies gl(n,ℂ) as the Lie algebra of GL(n,ℂ).

annotations.label: GL(n,C) is connected with maximal compact U(n)

anchor.snippet: Unlike the real case,

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Matrix.unitaryGroup` but no connectedness or maximal-compact statement for GL(n,ℂ).

annotations.label: Order of GL(n,q)

anchor.snippet: The order of

annotations.note: `Matrix.card_GL_field : Nat.card (GL (Fin n) 𝔽) = ∏ i, (q^n - q^i)` is the standard order formula.

annotations.label: GL(3,2) has order 168

anchor.snippet: For example, GL(3, 2) has order

annotations.note: No specific numerical instance for `Nat.card (GL (Fin 3) (ZMod 2)) = 168` was found, but it would follow from `Matrix.card_GL_field`.

annotations.label: Counting points of Grassmannian over F_q

anchor.snippet: More generally, one can count points of Grassmannian over

annotations.note: No Grassmannian point-counting formula (Gaussian binomial) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric group as GL over field with one element

anchor.snippet: In the philosophy of the field with one element

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize 'field with one element' nor the heuristic identifying Sₙ as GL(n, F₁).

annotations.label: Special linear group SL(n,F)

anchor.snippet: The special linear group ,

annotations.note: `Matrix.SpecialLinearGroup n R := { A // A.det = 1 }`, with notation `SL(n, R)`.

annotations.label: Determinant homomorphism with kernel SL

anchor.snippet: then the determinant is a group homomorphism

annotations.note: The determinant is formalized as the monoid hom `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup.det : GL n R →* Rˣ`, but no explicit lemma identifies its kernel with `SpecialLinearGroup`.

annotations.label: GL as semidirect product of SL and F*

anchor.snippet: can be written as a semidirect product

annotations.note: No semidirect-product decomposition `GL n F ≃ SL n F ⋊ Fˣ` is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SL is the derived group of GL

anchor.snippet: The special linear group is also the derived group

annotations.note: No theorem identifying `SpecialLinearGroup` with the commutator subgroup of `GeneralLinearGroup` was found.

annotations.label: SL is a Lie subgroup of dimension n²-1

anchor.snippet: is a Lie subgroup of

annotations.note: No Lie-subgroup structure or dimension statement for SL is registered in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SL as volume- and orientation-preserving transformations

anchor.snippet: can be characterized as the group of volume and orientation-preserving linear transformations

annotations.note: No characterization of SL(n,ℝ) as volume- and orientation-preserving linear maps was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simple connectedness of SL(n,C)

anchor.snippet: is simply connected, while

annotations.note: No fundamental-group computation for SL(n,ℂ) or SL(n,ℝ) is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invertible diagonal matrices subgroup

anchor.snippet: The set of all invertible diagonal matrices forms a subgroup

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Matrix.diagonal` but no explicit subgroup of invertible diagonal matrices inside `GL n R`.

annotations.label: Scalar matrix

anchor.snippet: A scalar matrix is a diagonal matrix which is a constant times the identity matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.scalar n : α →+* Matrix n n α` sends `a` to the diagonal matrix `a · I`.

annotations.label: Center of SL(n,F)

anchor.snippet: The center of

annotations.note: `center_equiv_rootsOfUnity` exhibits Z(SL(n,R)) as the n-th roots of unity (scalar matrices `ζ·I` with `ζⁿ = 1`).

annotations.label: Classical groups

anchor.snippet: The so-called classical groups are subgroups of

annotations.note: Individual classical groups (`orthogonalGroup`, `unitaryGroup`, `symplecticGroup`, `SpecialLinearGroup`) are defined, but Mathlib has no umbrella notion 'classical group'.

annotations.label: Orthogonal group

anchor.snippet: which preserves a non-degenerate quadratic form on

annotations.note: `Matrix.orthogonalGroup n R` is the submonoid of matrices with `A * Aᵀ = 1`.

annotations.label: Symplectic group

anchor.snippet: which preserves a symplectic form on

annotations.note: `symplecticGroup l R` is the submonoid of matrices preserving the standard symplectic form `J`.

annotations.label: Unitary group

anchor.snippet: preserves a non-degenerate hermitian form on

annotations.note: `Matrix.unitaryGroup n α := unitary (Matrix n n α)` — matrices with `A * star A = 1`.

annotations.label: Projective linear group

anchor.snippet: are the quotients of

annotations.note: `Matrix.ProjGenLinGroup n R := GL n R ⧸ Subgroup.center (GL n R)` with notation `PGL(n, R)`.

annotations.label: Affine group

anchor.snippet: is an extension of

annotations.note: `AffineEquiv P₁ P₁` has a group structure (the affine group), but Mathlib has no dedicated `AffineGroup` name nor the SES `1 → V → Aff(V) → GL(V) → 1`.

annotations.label: Special affine group and Poincaré group

anchor.snippet: for instance, the special affine group is the subgroup defined by the semidirect product

annotations.note: Neither the special affine group nor the Poincaré group is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General semilinear group

anchor.snippet: is the group of all invertible semilinear transformations

annotations.note: No `GeneralSemilinearGroup` / ΓL is defined in Mathlib, although semilinear maps exist.

annotations.label: Semilinear transformation

anchor.snippet: A semilinear transformation is a transformation which is linear

annotations.note: Mathlib's `LinearMap` is parameterized by a ring hom `σ : R →+* S` (notation `→ₛₗ[σ]`), encoding semilinear maps; the equivalence version is `LinearEquiv` (`≃ₛₗ`).

annotations.label: Projective semilinear group as collineation group

anchor.snippet: is the collineation group of projective space

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define PΓL or characterize it as the collineation group of projective space.

annotations.label: Full linear monoid

anchor.snippet: the resulting algebraic structure is a monoid , usually called the full linear monoid

annotations.note: `Module.End R M := M →ₗ[R] M` (and `Matrix n n R`) carry a monoid/ring structure, but there is no dedicated `FullLinearMonoid` named declaration.

annotations.label: Infinite general linear group

anchor.snippet: The infinite general linear group or stable general linear group is the direct limit

annotations.note: No stable / infinite general linear group (direct limit `GL = colim GL(n)`) is defined in Mathlib.
